rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al VS. HAYLEE YEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Receiver'S Motion For Instructions Regarding Tehama Street Properties
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, line 1. OTHER E. LYNN SCHOENMANN's Notice Of Hearing On Receiver'S Motion For Instructions Regarding Tehama Street Properties Continued to April 24, 2014 for supplemental briefing. Receiver shall obtain bids from three licensed contractors regarding remediation of (1) outstanding Notices of Violation items, (2) "habitability" items as defined and specified by Receiver; (3) "safety" items as defined and specified by Receiver; and (4) "public nuisance" items as defined and specified by Receiver. Receiver shall file a supplemental brief not exceeding 5 pages, excluding declarations and exhibits no later than April 11, 2014. Defendants shall file supplemental response not exceeding 5 pages, excluding declarations and exhibits no later than April 18, 2014. = (501/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: CORY NICOLAI VS. FRANK WINDOM et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT CITIMORTGAGE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurer to the cause of action for quiet title is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts as to the discovery of the facts supporting claim; demurrer to the causes of action for Permanent injunction is sustained with leave to amend to re-plead as remedy; demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is overruled. = (501/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: JAHZEEL RODRIQUEZ et al VS. ETHELJIVE GUEVARA, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT ETHELJIVE GUEVARA's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Plaintiff presented sufficient evidence regarding probability of prevailing on the merits. The Court neither 'weigh[s] credibility [nor] compare[s] the weight of the evidence. Rather, [we] accept as true the evidence favorable to the plaintiff [citation] and evaluate the defendant's evidence only to determine if it has defeated that submitted by the plaintiff as a matter of law.' [Citation.]" Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1536.= (501/RAK)
